steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states that UNESCO chooses sites for their cultural or natural value to preserve for future generations. The second sentence gives a specific case — Kiribati's coral ecosystem being added — as a direct result of that criterion.

steps.title: Apply the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. The blank needs a transition signaling that Kiribati's addition followed logically from the stated selection criterion.
  • Choice A — "By contrast,": signals opposition, but Kiribati's addition is consistent with, not contrary to, UNESCO's stated mission. ✗
  • Choice B — "Next,": signals the next item in a sequence, but the second sentence isn't a sequential step — it's an outcome of the criterion. ✗
  • Choice C — "In other words,": signals a restatement, but Kiribati's specific addition is a new fact, not a rephrasing of the selection criterion. ✗
  • Choice D — "Accordingly,": signals that something follows as a logical result, so fits: Kiribati was added because it met the stated criterion. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
